evidence_quotes.quote: I have personally experienced a game that I spent money on being shut down, leaving me unable to play it.
Publishers should not leave them in a completely unplayable state.
In fact, around 1.5 million people have already signed a petition requesting that video games not be destroyed, so clearly there is a lot of demand for consumer rights in the video game industry too.

paragraphs.text: Hello there, as a German citizen, I think something related should also be added to the initiative, to make it fairer for consumers. Since the Digital Fairness Act already covers gambling-like features in games, it would be fitting to extend this to the ownership of games. Just because it's a game doesn't mean you shouldn't be able to own it fairly. Since digital fairness is the topic of the initiative, I don't see why video games, which are also digital, should be excluded from it. In fact, around 1.5 million people have already signed a petition requesting that video games not be destroyed, so clearly there is a lot of demand for consumer rights in the video game industry too. Customers should be protected from publishers destroying video games. Video games also contain a lot of art, history and culture, which is another reason why I think they should be protected.I have personally experienced a game that I spent money on being shut down, leaving me unable to play it. I did not find that fair. There are hundreds of other games that have already been destroyed, and more will be destroyed. Publishers should not leave them in a completely unplayable state. I hope the EU Commission will consider my feedback and make video game ownership fairer.
